## ACME.BOT Review

After using ACME.BOT for several weeks, I found it to be incredibly user-friendly and efficient. The tool automates keyword research, which made discovering high-traffic keywords effortless. Its ability to generate well-researched articles impressed me, especially since the content felt authoritative and engaging. The visual aspect, with its custom illustration generator, added a professional touch that enhanced my blog posts. Publishing was a breeze with the one-click feature, and it integrated smoothly with my WordPress site. Overall, ACME.BOT simplified my content process without sacrificing quality, making it ideal for busy marketers seeking reliable automation.

## Key Features

## Pricing Plans

The subscription starts at $49 per month, providing access to all features. It promises to replace costly tools and freelance expenses, making it an economical choice for content marketers and small businesses.

## Wrap up

Overall, ACME.BOT is a powerful, all-in-one content creation tool that can save you time and money. It’s especially suitable for those who want quality, SEO-optimized content without the hassle of manual creation. Whether you’re a blogger, marketer, or small business owner, this platform offers great value—just be prepared to spend some time learning its features for the best results.

# Your blog on autopilot. Written with your expertise.

ACME.BOT is an AI blog agent that runs your blog from research to publish. It interviews you first, so every post carries the take only you have.

Start free trial

no credit card100 free creditsno sales call

Abhishek Iyer, maker of ACME.BOT

Abhishek Iyer

Made by anex-Google Search engineer.

“Running experiments on how search engines rank is my bread and butter, so when I couldn't find a tool that used AI to write genuinely helpful content, I built my own.”

as featured on

Featured on New York MagazineFeatured on The Information

Most AI blogs sound the same.

They read like an average of Wikipedia.

Readers scroll past. Search engines bury them.

Yours won't.

Most AI blogs read like Wikipedia. Yours won't. [...] Most AI blogs read like Wikipedia. Yours won't.

Most AI blogsread likeWikipedia.Yours won't.

## Why would any answer engine rely on a page it could have produced itself?

Google has a name for what survives.NON-COMMODITY CONTENT.

 Experience: first-hand, lived detail
 Expertise: your knowledge, cited
 Authoritativeness: sourced, fact-checked
 Trust: transparent + reviewed

Built around Google's E-E-A-T by an ex-Google search engineer.

A hand-lettered cardboard sign reading 'no AI slop', with a crossed-out symbol

HOW IT WORKS

## ACME.BOT interviews you before it writes.

It does the work. You just weigh in with the take only you have.

1

Researches the web

Deep research on the topic, competitors, and what's already ranking.

2

Reads your world [...] What platforms do you work with?

WordPress and Shopify, with the blog included free on Shopify. Connect your site once, and internal links, meta tags, and schema markup are generated automatically on every publish. On other platforms, get in touch; more integrations are on the roadmap.

Does ACME.BOT get better over time?

Yes. Interview answers, feedback, and documents accumulate in its knowledge base, so later articles need less input and match your voice faster. Feedback saves as permanent rules at the business, agent, or post level.

What is an AI blog agent?

results.title: Acme Tools is rated "Bad" with 1.5 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content: I ordered a birthday present for my husband that was over $500.---Hard saved for money for me. They sent a ship notice with Fedex tracking. Item never arrived the day specified and I couldn't get info from Fedex despite trying daily. I finally emailed Acme just to let them know that there was no tracking update info. I was told that IF my order did not arrive in 3 days I should "take it up with Paypal"!!! (I used paypal to pay). I was gobsmacked. So, they figured that my not receiving what I ordered and paid for was not their problem??? I just never had any reputable company do this to me before. I knew then that I could not trust them, and I called Paypal immediately and read them the email from Acme. They too thought it was pretty bad. They were great, and said that IF I did not receive [...] ## Horrible customer service [...] ## False advertising, bait and switch or a mistake? You decide
